pr.title: Move lbcommand out of asm with some attempted non-matching
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Moved the lbcommand unit from a standalone asm object into src/melee/lb/lbcommand.c, deleting asm/melee/lb/lbcommand.s and removing its asm object from obj_files.mk. The PR kept many routines as inline asm in C while adding attempted NON_MATCHING C versions for some command handlers. It also renamed the exported dispatcher from func_80005B64 to Command_80005B64 at known asm callsites, expanded lbcommand.h prototypes, and refined CommandInfo field naming from unk_x10 to event_return.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/lb/lbcommand.s, which previously contained lbcommand text, the lbl_803B9840 command function table, and lbl_804D79E8 sdata2 constants.

Added most of the deleted assembly into src/melee/lb/lbcommand.c as inline asm functions using asm void and nofralloc, preserving instruction-by-instruction behavior while moving ownership to the C translation unit.

Added a lbl_803B9840 function-pointer table in C with 16 entries: Command_00, Command_04, Command_08, Command_0C, Command_10_ExecuteLoop, Command_10_Goto, Command_80005AC4, Command_80005AE4, Command_80005B00, Command_80005B18, followed by NULL entries.
Added attempted C implementations under #ifdef NON_MATCHING for Command_0C, Command_10_Goto, and Command_80005AC4 while keeping matching inline asm versions in the default build.
Renamed references to the dispatcher func_80005B64 as Command_80005B64 in asm callsites in ft/code_8006DBF4.s, it/code_80266F3C.s, and lb/code_8000F9F8.s.
Updated lbcommand.h to include global.h and dolphin/types.h, renamed CommandInfo.unk_x10 to event_return, and added prototypes for the moved command handlers.
Updated include/functions.h comments/prototypes to use Command_80005B64 and expose func_80021C48() for the inline asm call from Command_80005B18.
Removed $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/lb/lbcommand.o from TEXT_O_FILES because lbcommand now builds from src/melee/lb/lbcommand.o.

Inserted an extra .4byte NULL in asm/melee/lb/lbcollision.s sdata2 before lbl_804D79F0, likely to preserve data layout after moving lbcommand constants, though the diff alone does not explain the precise linker/layout reason.
smart_moves: Migrated the object out of asm without requiring all functions to match immediately, using inline asm as a bridge so the file could live in src while preserving behavior.
Kept non-matching C attempts guarded by NON_MATCHING, making the decompilation reasoning available for future work without risking the matching build.

Gave semantic names to several command handlers, especially Command_0C, Command_10_ExecuteLoop, and Command_10_Goto, while preserving address-based names for less-understood routines such as Command_80005AC4.
Updated all known assembly callsites to the new dispatcher symbol name, avoiding split symbol usage between func_80005B64 and Command_80005B64.
Renamed a CommandInfo field from unk_x10 to event_return based on observed command-loop behavior, improving later readability without over-renaming every unknown field.
decomp_lessons: For early decomp work, moving an asm file into C can be valuable even when most routines remain inline asm; it centralizes prototypes, tables, and NON_MATCHING experiments in the source tree.
When converting a command interpreter, preserving the dispatch table order is critical. Here lbl_803B9840 retained 16 slots and NULL padding while mapping opcode-like entries to command functions.
Guard speculative C rewrites with #ifdef NON_MATCHING and keep a matching inline asm fallback until instruction output is confirmed.
If a moved asm unit contained data or sdata2 constants, check neighboring object layout and section padding. This PR had to compensate in lbcollision.s with a NULL word after lbcommand.s deletion.
Renaming externally-called symbols requires updating raw asm callsites and broad prototype/comment registries, not only the C file that defines the symbol.
Field names can be advanced incrementally when supported by usage patterns; event_return is more useful than unk_x10 for loop/goto command analysis, while other unknown fields were left conservative.
naming_conventions: Command handlers use the Command_XX form for apparent command/opcode values, such as Command_00, Command_04, Command_08, and Command_0C.
Behavioral suffixes were used where the same apparent command group needed clarification: Command_10_ExecuteLoop and Command_10_Goto.
Unmatched or not-yet-understood handlers retained address-based names with a Command_ prefix, such as Command_80005AC4, Command_80005AE4, Command_80005B00, Command_80005B18, and Command_80005B64.
The dispatcher formerly known as func_80005B64 was renamed to Command_80005B64, signaling that it belongs to the command subsystem rather than being a generic unknown function.
CommandInfo member unk_x10 was renamed to event_return based on its use as a saved data_position/return target in loop and goto handling.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used inline asm functions with nofralloc to transplant exact assembly from asm/melee/lb/lbcommand.s into src/melee/lb/lbcommand.c.
Used #ifdef NON_MATCHING blocks to keep attempted C source for Command_0C, Command_10_Goto, and Command_80005AC4 while defaulting to matching inline asm.
Preserved labels inside inline asm, such as lbl_80005A6C, lbl_80005B9C, and lbl_80005BA0, where branches depended on local labels.

Preserved the indirect dispatch sequence in Command_80005B64: bounds check against 0x0A, index into lbl_803B9840, load function pointer into r12, mtctr/mtlr-style branch via blrl in the original listing, return 1 for handled commands and 0 otherwise.
Represented the former lbl_804D79E8 float constant use in Command_80005B00 as an inline asm lfs of 3.4028235E38 rather than relying on the old sdata2 symbol.
Added a NULL word in lbcollision.s sdata2, suggesting section alignment or small-data layout needed manual preservation after deleting lbcommand.s.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match Command_0C, Command_10_Goto, Command_80005AC4, Command_80005AE4, Command_80005B00, Command_80005B18, or Command_80005B64.
Search for later renames or type refinements of CommandInfo fields data_position, loop_count, event_return, loop_count_dup, and unk_x18.
Investigate why lbcollision.s needed an inserted .4byte NULL after deleting lbcommand.s and whether later linker-section cleanup removed that workaround.
Search for all remaining callsites of Command_80005B64 and whether its signature was later corrected to include the command id argument.
Compare this PR with later asm-to-C migrations that use inline asm plus NON_MATCHING blocks as an intermediate step.
